dropped_below_cutoff.reason: Defense-in-depth gap in report XML var substitution → ExpressionLanguage, but function allowlist = [stristr] and string-literal escape correct. Laravel query-builder bound params prevent SQLi in filter paths. No exploitable sink today.
Highest input-density module (48 Validator call-sites) but zero dangerous filesystem operations (grep-verified). Defer to future structured audit.
Editor-only path construction via Flysystem, which blocks traversal. Related to SEC-AUDIT-002 follow-up.

new_t0_signals.note: Previous run's dangerous_count=3 was a `system(` false positive matching `mediaFilesystem` substring; corrected by word-boundary regex in new T0 scanner. Fix commit b2dc869b90 is intact; no regression.

deduped_against_existing.note: MediaFileService upload-allowlist spin-off remains queued. T1 reconfirmed the gap is still present in current MediaFileService.php.
replacementImageResponse CSP header gap — unchanged, still queued.
SVG serve-path enumeration — unchanged, still queued.

summary: No new tasks created. The visitor/member/editor attack surface reviewed in T1 shows consistent Validator + bound-parameter + access-check patterns. Only defense-in-depth observations recorded in priorities.md.
